entries.content: Prefer the simplest solution that works. Optimise for readability and change; clever code is a liability. YAGNI — don't build what you don't yet need.
Reproduce reliably, read the actual error, form one hypothesis, test it, change one thing at a time. Bisect to isolate. The bug is usually where you're most sure it isn't.
Test behaviour, not implementation. Cover the highest-impact paths and edge cases first. A failing test that reproduces a bug is the fastest way to fix it and prevent regression.
Low coupling, high cohesion. Depend on abstractions. Keep the dependency graph acyclic and the blast radius of change small. Name things for what they mean.
